_description: RFC §11 conformance corpus for the coupling-library / coupling_import feature (docs/content/rfcs/coupling-libraries-role-binding.md). Follows the tests/invalid/expected_errors.json convention: a map from fixture filename to its expected outcome. `pass` fixtures load/flatten/expand cleanly; `fail` fixtures raise the named esm-spec §10.11 diagnostic `code` via the listed `driver`. Kept in a dedicated directory so the other bindings' auto-globbed valid/ and invalid/ suites are undisturbed.

rothermel_fuel.esm.note: Well-formed coupling library (RFC §6): schema-valid, load()s clean, and save()->load() round-trips (esm-spec §10.9). Also the regression guard for the §9 root-`anyOf` `coupling_roles` branch — a bare roles+coupling document passes root-schema validation.

assembly_import.esm.note: Imports rothermel_fuel.esm; flatten() deep-equals flatten(assembly_inline.esm) (equivalence — pure sugar over the coupling layer, esm-spec §10.10.3).

assembly_inline.esm.note: The substituted form of assembly_import.esm (five inline variable_map edges). The equivalence partner.

assembly_multi.esm.note: Two coupling_import edges of one library with distinct binds -> two independent, non-interfering edge sets (multiple instantiation, RFC §6/§11).

full_surface_lib.esm.note: Exercises every §10.10.2 occurrence site (variable_map apply_expression_template bindings values, couple systems+connector, operator_compose translate keys+values, event conditions/affects/discrete_parameters).

assembly_full_surface.esm.note: Imports full_surface_lib.esm; expandCouplingImports rewrites every role occurrence (no residual role names in the expanded edges).

plain_model.esm.note: An ordinary model document (no coupling_roles). Not itself under test; the non-library import target for import_not_library.esm.

import_misbind_downstream.esm.note: Binding is structurally complete, so expandCouplingImports SUCCEEDS; the mis-bind (Spread -> RothermelNoW0, which lacks `w0`) surfaces on the expanded edge as the existing unresolved_scoped_ref (esm-spec §4.1/§7). KNOWN TS GAP: the TS reference `flatten()` does not itself re-run scoped-ref resolution on expanded edges (that check lives in validate.ts, which iterates the UN-expanded coupling), so the test drives it by validating the expanded coupling — proving 'a bad binding is just a bad edge, caught where all bad edges are.' The gap is reported, not patched.

coupletype_model.esm.note: Model carries the removed `coupletype`; Model is additionalProperties:false, so schema validation fails (unknown property).

coupletype_reaction_system.esm.note: ReactionSystem carries the removed `coupletype`; additionalProperties:false schema failure.
